title: Tamil Cabbage Poriyal

subs.name: carrot, finely grated — reduce cooking time to 5 minutes
2 tbsp desiccated coconut soaked for 5 minutes in warm water

steps.text: Heat oil in a kadai over medium-high flame. Splutter mustard seeds, then add urad dal and fry until it turns light golden. Add curry leaves, shallots, and green chillies and sauté for 2 minutes until the shallots soften.
Add the shredded cabbage and turmeric. Toss well to coat in the tempering. Sprinkle a pinch of salt — this draws out just enough moisture to help the cabbage cook without added water.

Stir-fry on medium-high heat for 8–10 minutes, tossing the cabbage frequently, until it is cooked through but still holds a slight bite. It should look glossy and shrunk to about half its original volume.

Check salt and remove from heat. Scatter the grated coconut over the cabbage and toss once more. Serve as a side dish with rice, sambar, and rasam — the poriyal is meant to be the dry contrast to the gravies.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: cabbage → carrot, finely grated — reduce cooking time to 5 minutes; coconut → 2 tbsp desiccated coconut soaked for 5 minutes in warm water; shallot → 1 small red onion, finely chopped
